nav_airdrop_desc: Send tokens to all holders of another token
footer_tagline_before: A market-cap & analytics site for BCH CashTokens. Visit
footer_tagline_after: to learn more about Bitcoin Cash.
metrics_tracked_tip: Every CashToken category our indexer has ever seen since activation block 792,772 — fungible and non-fungible, active or long-dead. Minus the ones hidden by moderation.
metrics_listed_tip: Subset of Tracked — tokens you can buy or sell right now via at least one venue: an active Cauldron or Fex AMM price, or an open Tapswap P2P listing. Click the number to filter the directory to just these.
metrics_listed_title: Tokens with an active Cauldron / Fex AMM price or an open Tapswap P2P listing
metrics_tvl_tip: BCH-side reserve across every Cauldron + Fex pool, priced in USD. Cauldron portion is the canonical aggregate from indexer.cauldron.quest (refreshed every 30 min); Fex portion is summed across every pool we scan on-chain. Single-side (conservative; industry convention doubles this). Tapswap excluded — P2P intent, not pooled liquidity.
metrics_new24h_tip: Categories whose genesis transaction was mined in the last 24 hours. Click to view them.
metrics_new24h_title: Tokens minted in the last 24 hours
metrics_txs24h_tip: CashToken-bearing transactions across all categories in the last 24 hours — every block tx that emits at least one token output (transfers, mints, multi-token movements; each tx counts once). Pure on-chain count from the verbose block JSON. Click for the FAQ explainer.
metrics_txs24h_title: CashToken-bearing txs across all categories, last 24h
metrics_block_tip: Latest BCH block our tail worker has scanned for new CashToken categories and Tapswap listings. Typically sub-second behind tip via ZMQ. Click to open the per-block economics dashboard.
metrics_bch_tip: BCH spot price in USD, refreshed every 5 minutes. Used across the site to convert BCH-denominated prices (Cauldron, Tapswap) to USD.
cta_heading: Follow Panmoni on X for BCH & CashTokens updates
cta_sub: The team behind TokenStork — product launches, dev notes, and ecosystem signal.
error_404_subline: The URL you tried doesn't match anything on the site. If you pasted a category hex, double-check the 64 characters — one wrong digit is a different token.
error_410_subline: This token category was removed from the directory by moderation. We keep the list of removals public on /moderated for transparency.
error_500_subline: Something went wrong on our end. We're probably already looking at it; if not, refresh in a minute.
error_410_footer_after: for the full list of hidden categories with reason and date.
meta_title: Token Stork: Discover, Track and Analyze BCH Cash Tokens
meta_description: Track BCH CashTokens market cap and more with TokenStork.
login_h1: Sign in with your BCH wallet
login_intro: No email, no password. Connect via WalletConnect — your wallet handles the cryptography; we just verify the signature and issue a session.
login_wallet_support_before: Works with Cashonize, Paytaca, Zapit, and any other wallet that supports the
login_manual_hint: For users without a WalletConnect-aware wallet — paste your cashaddr and a signed-message signature.
login_wc_awaiting: Approve the connection in your wallet. The QR code modal should be open above this page.
login_wc_signing: Approve the message signature in your wallet…
login_cashaddr_hint: Mainnet P2PKH only. The cashaddr you enter must match the address whose private key you'll sign with.
login_step1: Step 1 — Sign this message in your wallet
login_how_to_sign: How to sign in popular wallets
login_wallet_ec_steps: Tools → Sign / Verify Message → paste address + message → Sign.
login_wallet_cashonize_steps: Settings → Sign Message → choose your wallet → paste message → Sign.
login_wallet_paytaca_steps: Settings → Sign Message → select wallet → paste message → Sign.
login_copy_sig_before: Copy the resulting signature (a base64 string ending in
login_why_p1: The address you sign with becomes your account. We store nothing the wallet didn't already prove you control — no email, no recovery phone, no password to lose.
login_why_p2: This unlocks the wallet-tied watchlist, future portfolio tracking, and per-token annotations. Until those land, login doesn't gate any of the directory data — it's all public.
login_err_not_configured: WalletConnect is not configured on this deployment. Set PUBLIC_WALLETCONNECT_PROJECT_ID and rebuild, or sign manually below.
pubbcmr_meta_description: Publish or update a Bitcoin Cash Metadata Registry (BCMR) entry for a CashToken category whose authority NFT you hold.
pubbcmr_intro_before: Give your CashToken a name, symbol, icon, and description by publishing a Bitcoin Cash Metadata Registry entry on-chain. Only the wallet holding a category's
pubbcmr_intro_after: can publish — that's how the protocol prevents impersonation.
pubbcmr_indexer_err_body: We use it to find which categories your wallet can publish for. Refresh to retry — this usually clears in seconds.
pubbcmr_abandon_confirm: Abandon this draft? You can always start a new one.
pubbcmr_no_eligible_body1: This wallet doesn't currently hold the authority NFT for any category we've indexed.
pubbcmr_no_eligible_body2_before: If you just minted a token, give the indexer a moment to pick it up and refresh. Or
pubbcmr_no_eligible_body2_after: — after minting, return here to publish its BCMR.
bcmrw_s1_desc_before: What this token is called and how it appears in wallets + explorers. These fields go into the BCMR JSON as the registry's
bcmrw_upload_hint: Pre-fills the form from an existing BCMR JSON file
bcmrw_decimals_hint: 0 for indivisible (NFT-style or whole-units), up to 8 for finely-divisible.
bcmrw_description_ph: A brief explanation of this token's purpose.
bcmrw_v_name_long: Name must be 80 characters or fewer.
bcmrw_v_ticker_long: Ticker must be 12 characters or fewer.
bcmrw_v_decimals: Decimals must be an integer in 0-8.
bcmrw_v_desc_long: Description must be 500 characters or fewer.
bcmrw_s2_desc_a: Where to find your token's icon image. Paste an
bcmrw_s2_desc_c: reference. The image should be a square PNG or WebP at 256×256 or larger. Leave blank to publish without an icon.
bcmrw_v_icon_long: Icon URI must be 1024 characters or fewer.
bcmrw_v_icon_scheme: Icon URI must start with https://, http://, or ipfs:// (or leave blank).
bcmrw_s3_desc_before: We'll build the canonical BCMR JSON from your inputs and compute its
bcmrw_s3_desc_after: — that hash will be committed on-chain alongside the publication URI, so any consumer can verify the JSON bytes match what you intended to publish.
bcmrw_ipfs_key_ph: Pinata JWT or Lighthouse API key
bcmrw_ipfs_key_note: Saved to localStorage — never sent to Token Stork.
bcmrw_err_enter_pinata: Enter your Pinata API key below.
bcmrw_s4_desc_before: Upload the canonical JSON from step 3 to your own IPFS / Pinata / Lighthouse / any HTTPS host you control, then paste the URL here.
bcmrw_s4_desc_strong: Your own host stays the canonical source
bcmrw_pub_url_hint: HTTPS or ipfs:// accepted. If your host uses redirects, paste the final URL. Maximum 8 MiB body.
bcmrw_verify_match: Hosted bytes match the canonical content hash.
bcmrw_verify_mismatch_help: The bytes returned by your host don't match what step 3 canonicalized. Re-download the canonical JSON from step 3 and upload exactly those bytes (any whitespace difference changes the hash).
bcmrw_backup_title: Optional: submit a backup copy to tokenstork.com.
bcmrw_backup_body_after: after operator approval, so consumers can fall back to our copy if your own host is ever unreachable. Your IPFS / HTTPS host remains the canonical source — this is just an additional mirror. Approval is operator-discretionary.
bcmrw_backup_ok: ✓ Backup submitted. The operator will review; approval is independent of the on-chain publication, which proceeds either way.
bcmrw_s5_desc_before: We build the unsigned transaction that spends your category's authority NFT, emits a new authority NFT to your wallet, and attaches an
bcmrw_s5_desc_after: locator. Token Stork can orchestrate the signing via WalletConnect — or you can build, sign manually, and paste the hex below.
bcmrw_checking_wallet_long: Checking your wallet for sufficient BCH and the authority NFT…
bcmrw_consolidate_hint: Consolidate plain BCH into one spendable UTXO
bcmrw_resolve_issues: Resolve the issues above to enable one-click signing, or use the manual flow below.
bcmrw_click_check: Click "Check wallet" to see if you're ready to publish.
bcmrw_sign_hex_hint: Sign this hex in your wallet (Paytaca, Electron Cash, Cashonize, etc.). Then paste the signed hex below.
bcmrw_signed_hex_ph: Paste signed hex from your wallet here…
bcmrw_err_paste_hex: Paste the signed tx hex from your wallet first.
bcmrw_err_hex_format: Signed hex must be even-length and hex-only (0-9, a-f).
bcmrw_err_unsigned: This is the unsigned transaction. Sign it in your wallet first.
bcmrw_s6_body: The transaction is in the mempool. Our on-chain BCMR walker runs hourly — your token's name, symbol, icon, and description will start appearing on the directory within ~1 hour of the next walker tick after this tx confirms.
bcmrw_s6_placeholder: This step shows after a successful broadcast. Complete step 5 first.
bcmrw_abandon_confirm: Abandon this draft? You can start a fresh one for this category afterwards.
airdrop_intro_before: Send tokens you hold to every wallet that holds another token. Equal split or proportional to recipient holdings. Built on local
airdrop_s1_q: Which of YOUR tokens are you airdropping?
airdrop_no_tokens: Your wallet doesn't currently hold any non-moderated CashTokens. Receive some on this address (or wait for the next 6h enrich tick if you just received a token) and come back.
airdrop_in_wallet_suffix: in your wallet. Click one to airdrop:
airdrop_paste_hex_summary: Or paste a category hex directly
airdrop_err_not_hold: You don't currently hold this token.
airdrop_s2_q: Whose holders should receive? Type a name, symbol, or paste a category hex. They'll all get a share of your tokens.
airdrop_search_label: Search by name, symbol, or hex
airdrop_equal_desc: Every holder gets the same amount.
airdrop_weighted_desc: Proportional to each holder's balance + NFT count of the recipient token.
airdrop_dust_hint: Default 800. Below 800: recipients may need to add their own funding to spend the token UTXO. Below 546: rejected by standard relays.
airdrop_signed_hex_ph: Paste the signed transaction hex here
airdrop_err_hex_even: Signed hex must be even-length hex.
airdrop_err_snapshot_advanced: Recipient holder set changed mid-airdrop. Halting; visit the receipt page to redraft remaining chunks.
mint_meta_description: Mint your own BCH CashToken (FT, NFT, or hybrid) directly from your wallet.
mint_intro: Create your own fungible token, NFT, or hybrid on the Bitcoin Cash chain. Walk through the six-step wizard, sign the genesis transaction with your wallet, and your category appears on the directory within minutes.
mint_auth_body: Minting a CashToken means signing an on-chain transaction with the BCH address that owns the funding UTXO. We don't store your private key, we don't email you, and we don't ask for any other identity — your wallet IS the account.
mint_auth_lost_key: Lost key = lost account. That's an intended property; not a bug.
mint_save_failed: Couldn't save your draft on the last step transition — your progress is in memory only. A refresh will lose it. Next save attempt will retry automatically.
mint_s1_desc: CashTokens has three flavors. The type is part of the genesis transaction and can't be changed once minted.
mint_type_ft_desc: A divisible token with a fixed total supply, like USD or BTC. Choose this for currencies, governance tokens, points.
mint_type_nft_desc: Each token is unique with optional commitment data. Choose this for art, collectibles, identity claims.
mint_type_hybrid_desc: Both at once. The minting NFT controls future FT issuance — common for governance + treasury tokens.
mint_s2_desc: What this token is called and what it looks like. Will be published as BCMR metadata so wallets and explorers display it consistently.
mint_description_ph: A sentence or two for the BCMR metadata.
mint_icon_uri_ph: https://… or ipfs://… — leave empty to publish without an icon
mint_pin_icon_summary: Pin an icon file to IPFS
mint_pin_icon_desc_a: Upload a static raster (PNG / JPEG / WebP, ≤ 2 MiB) directly from your browser to
mint_pin_icon_desc_c: using your own API key. The file never reaches Token Stork's server. Once pinned, the resulting
mint_pin_icon_desc_d: populates the Icon URI field above.
mint_s3_ft_desc_a: The total supply minted at genesis. This is the only mint event unless you keep the minting NFT (FT+NFT hybrid). Up to 2
mint_cap_none: None — pure NFT, can't mint or mutate
mint_s4_desc_b: is the txid of a UTXO you own at
mint_s4_desc_c: We'll auto-detect suitable UTXOs from your wallet below. The recipient is automatically set to your authenticated address.
mint_checking_utxos: Checking your wallet for suitable UTXOs…
mint_paste_manual_note: You can still paste a txid manually below.
mint_plain_cta_rest: Consolidate into one vout=0 with one wallet tap.
mint_click_refresh: Click Refresh to scan your wallet.
mint_unsigned_note: Note: the input's unlocking script is empty here — your wallet fills it during signing. That's why the hex below is shorter than the typical 220+ byte signed tx.
mint_sign_then_a: Sign this in your wallet, then paste the
mint_s6_appear: Your token will appear on the directory within ~10 minutes (one block + sync-tail tick). Until then, the BCMR file below is what you publish so wallets and explorers know its identity.
mint_s6_download_a: Download the BCMR JSON below, host it at any HTTPS URL you control (or pin it to IPFS and use the
mint_s6_download_b: form), then submit it to BCMR registries so wallets pick up your token's identity.
mint_v_pick_type: Pick a token type to continue.
mint_v_ticker_long: Ticker must be 12 characters or fewer.
mint_v_decimals: Decimals must be 0–8 per CashTokens spec.
mint_v_supply_required: Total supply is required for fungible tokens.
mint_v_supply_int: Total supply must be a whole number.
mint_v_commitment_hex: NFT commitment must be hex (0-9, a-f).
mint_v_commitment_len: NFT commitment max 40 bytes (80 hex chars).
mint_err_utxo_timeout: UTXO fetch timed out after 20 seconds. BlockBook may be slow — try again.
mint_warn_outpoint_notfound: This txid was not found at vout=0 in your wallet. The genesis tx MUST spend vout=0 — if you control a different output of this transaction, it will not work. Send BCH to yourself first to create a vout=0 UTXO.
mint_warn_outpoint_unverified: This txid could not be verified against your wallet. Make sure it is the txid of a transaction where you control output #0 (vout=0).
mint_err_derive_lock: Could not derive locking script from your address: {detail}
mint_err_no_plain: No plain-BCH UTXOs available for consolidation.
mint_err_paste_signed: Paste your signed tx hex from the wallet.
mint_err_signed_hex: Signed tx must be hex (0-9, a-f).
mint_err_too_short: This hex is too short to be a signed transaction — it looks like the unsigned tx from step 4. Sign it in your wallet first, then paste the signed version.
mint_err_unsigned: This is the unsigned transaction. Sign it in your wallet first, then paste the signed hex here.
mint_discard_confirm: Discard this draft? You will lose any progress in the current wizard.
mint_err_icon_size: Icon must be ≤ 2 MiB (matches the icon-safety pipeline cap).
mint_err_icon_key: Paste your IPFS provider API key first (same widget as below).
mint_err_ipfs_key: Paste your IPFS provider API key first.
mint_mint_as_b: . The genesis tx will be signed by that address; it must hold a UTXO with enough BCH to cover the genesis output + fees (typically 2000-3000 sats).
about_meta_description: TokenStork is a market-cap site for BCH CashTokens, built and maintained by George Donnelly.
about_intro_1: TokenStork.com is a market-cap site for
about_feedback: I welcome your suggestions, criticisms, bug reports and general feedback any time. Let me know what functionality would be of assistance to you, and I will probably add it!
about_help_1: Want to build a dapp on BCH but need some technical help?
about_help_3: and I'll build what you need.
about_news_2: , the only remaining censorship-free BCH subreddit where everything related to BCH is on-topic.
about_registry_2: , a BCH Metadata Registry. PRs are welcome at our
about_support_body: TokenStork is self-funded. Ongoing costs include a ~$50/month VPS bill (where the archival BCH node, Postgres, indexer workers, and web app all live), domain registration, and my time. If the site is useful to you, please consider chipping in by donating to the address below. Thank you!
about_ack_intro: TokenStork is made possible through the open-source projects and APIs below. Thank you for your collaboration!
about_ack_cashtokens: — the consensus specification that brought fungible tokens and NFTs to Bitcoin Cash at the May 2023 upgrade; the foundation everything on this site is built on
about_ack_chipbcmr: — the on-chain metadata-registry standard our hourly authchain walker reads directly from each token's authchain (with sha256 verification of the publisher's JSON body against the on-chain locator)
about_ack_cauldron: — AMM protocol + public price/TVL indexer feeding our directory and per-token pages
about_ack_tapswap: — MPSW P2P marketplace protocol, reverse-engineered from the reference indexer so we detect listings entirely from our own BCHN
about_ack_blockbook: — per-category holder, UTXO, and NFT indexer, built on
about_ack_bchn_1: — the archival full node underpinning every on-chain read; a fork of
about_ack_bchn_3: — each only minor patches atop the last, honoring the long legacy of Bitcoin development
about_contact_2: and we'll be thrilled to assist you.
learn_meta_description: Tutorials and resources for building with BCH CashTokens.
learn_intro: To get started, here are foundational CashTokens tutorials. We'll be adding more resources to this page over time.
learn_about_bch_desc: — a concise primer on BCH's UTXO model, covenants, and why CashTokens exist.
learn_intro_ct_desc: — the official walkthrough of fungible and non-fungible tokens on BCH.
learn_pioneers_intro: Hands-on tutorials for minting and working with CashTokens end-to-end.
learn_cashscript_intro: CashScript is the high-level language most CashTokens projects use to write BCH smart contracts and covenants.
learn_cashscript_org_desc: — project homepage, installation, and full documentation.
learn_covenants_desc: — how to constrain how coins can be spent, the foundation of most on-chain BCH apps.
learn_video_explained_desc: — short overview of what CashScript is and why it matters (BCH Clips).
learn_ref_bcmr_desc: — the metadata standard behind the "BCMR metadata" card on every token page.
learn_suggest: Have a tutorial or resource to suggest? Email
roadmap_intro: TokenStork.com aims to be a market-cap site for BCH CashTokens and a comprehensive service provider for on-chain CashTokens operations and data. Working with CashTokens should be smooth and easy, and TokenStork.com aims to make it that way. Below is what's next.
roadmap_footer_1: Roadmap last refreshed 2026-06-20. If you'd like to see something on here that isn't yet — email
rm_p1_b1: Nightly pg_dump + offsite ship (Backblaze B2 / Hetzner Storage Box, ~€1/mo) with 7-daily / 4-weekly retention.
rm_p2_b1: Standalone airdrop page — paste/import any address list (manual entry, paste-many, CSV) and airdrop a chosen token to all of them in one tx.
rm_p2_b2: Set-algebra recipient builder: union / intersect / exclude across multiple categories + blocklists + watchlists.
rm_p2_b3: Block-height snapshots ("holders as of block 950,000") + multi-venue DEX-pool unwrapping so LP contracts don't eat budget meant for real holders.
rm_p2_b4: WalletConnect v2 direct-sign in-page (currently paste-signed-hex matching /mint).
rm_p3_b1: Wallet owners of a category can manage it via TokenStork: dividends, mint-NFT management, and minting additional supply for FT+NFT hybrids. (BCMR publish/update for tokens whose authority NFT you hold already ships at /publish-bcmr; mass distribution ships at /airdrops.)
rm_p3_b2: Import-existing-token flow for tokens minted elsewhere.
rm_p4_b1: Long-window (7d / 30d) gainers, losers, and TVL movers on /stats — the 24h windows are live; the longer windows are next now that enough price history has accumulated.
rm_p4_b2: Community-submitted tags for filtering the directory (stablecoin, memecoin, utility, DAO, etc.).
rm_p4_b3: NFT-aware Tapswap arbitrage — current /arbitrage is FT-only on the Tapswap side; NFT listings need per-commitment treatment.
rm_l1_b1: Portfolio with P&L: manual buy entries OR derived from on-chain holdings at the linked wallet via BlockBook.
rm_l1_b2: Per-user price alerts (wallet-delivered notification, or email-as-metadata-only — never used for login).
rm_l1_b3: Personal annotations on tokens ("this one was the rug").
rm_l2_b1: /defi page — BCH locked across covenant families (Cauldron, AnyHedge, Moria, BCH Bull, BCH Guru, Badgers, Emerald DAO, BCH PUMP). Headline "Total BCH locked in DeFi".
rm_l2_b2: /nft page — minted / max supply, mint price, mint revenue, floor price, holders, last mint, mints in the last 7 days.
rm_l2_b3: Backed by a hand-curated projects/issuers layer that also powers category filter chips.
rm_l2_b4: Wallet-support badges ("Listed in Zapit / Paytaca / Cashonize / Electron Cash") via periodic scrape.
rm_l3_b1: Create and update BCMR identities for people and organisations.
rm_l3_b2: Full audit log of metadata changes.
rm_l3_b3: Updates feed when tokens revise their BCMR data; "recently updated" + "listed in registry X" badges on token cards.
rm_l4_b1: Launch ICOs via form with accountability milestones + transparent treasury + investor voting.
rm_l5_b1: Lighthouse pass: responsive images, CLS, CSP/XSS hardening.
rm_l5_b2: Streaming + suspense + islands for parallelised page loads.
rm_l5_b3: A11y sweep: aria labels, keyboard nav, screen-reader trend summaries.
rm_l5_b4: Custom site themes; responsive breakpoint audit.
rm_l6_b3: Comments + reviews on tokens, dapps, NFT series.
rm_l6_b5: Heatmaps, embeddable widgets, public API documentation.
rm_l7_b1: Additional on-chain products — annuities, structured payouts.
rm_l7_b2: Outreach for RWA tokenisation partnerships on CashTokens.
faq_meta_description: Frequently asked questions about TokenStork, BCH CashTokens, and the directory's data sources.
faq_intro_1: Quick answers to the things readers most often want to know about TokenStork and the CashTokens ecosystem. Click any question to expand. If something's missing here, email
faq_footer_1: Didn't find what you were looking for? Email
faq_a1:
TokenStork is a market-cap and directory site for BCH CashTokens. We index every category ever minted since the CashTokens upgrade activated at block 792,772 in May 2023, and surface prices, holders, metadata, and trade venues.

Everything runs on a single archival BCH node + our own Postgres. No third-party blockchain indexer sits in the pipeline — the site is fully self-hosted.
faq_a2:
CashTokens is Bitcoin Cash's native token standard, activated in May 2023. It lets any BCH transaction create fungible tokens (like stablecoins or meme coins) or non-fungible tokens (collectibles, membership passes) directly in the base protocol — no smart-contract platform required, no separate ledger.

Every token shares a category, a 32-byte hex identifier derived from the transaction where the category was first minted. You'll see categories referenced throughout the site in that format.

The Learn page links to tutorials for minting your own.
faq_a3:
Both are ways to attach a name, symbol, and decimals to a CashTokens category. They answer different questions and use very different mechanisms:

  • BCMR (CHIP-BCMR) is the BCH ecosystem's metadata registry standard. Issuers publish a JSON file (icon, description, links, NFT types, extensions) at a URI, then commit the URI + a sha256 of the JSON body inside an on-chain OP_RETURN BCMR locator on their token's authchain. We walk that authchain ourselves directly from the chain (no third-party indexer) and verify the body's hash against the on-chain commit before showing the metadata anywhere on the site.
  • CRC-20 (crc20.cash) is a permissionless on-chain naming convention. The symbol, decimals, and name are encoded inside a 21-byte CashScript covenant in the token's genesis transaction. We detect it directly from chain data — no third-party registry, no publisher, no DNS dependency.

The big practical difference: BCMR is self-asserted (any issuer can declare any symbol they like in their JSON), while CRC-20 is first-come-first-served on chain, with one canonical winner per symbol determined by a deterministic sort (max(commit_block, reveal_block − 20), ties broken by category id). A token can have BCMR alone, CRC-20 alone, both, or neither. When both exist, the on-chain CRC-20 bytes win on authenticity (you can verify them against the genesis tx); BCMR wins on richness (icons, descriptions, social links).

Browse all CRC-20 tokens at /crc20 — the page splits canonical winners from non-canonical contenders so you can see where symbols are contested.
faq_q4: What does FT+NFT mean?
faq_a4:
CashToken categories come in three shapes based on the kinds of outputs the token appears on:

  • FT — fungible only. Every output has an amount but no NFT commitment. Tokens you count — balances are interchangeable. Example: a BCH-denominated stablecoin.
  • NFT — non-fungible only. Every output has an NFT commitment (arbitrary bytes identifying the unique item). No fungible balance. Example: a collectible series.
  • FT+NFT — hybrid. The same category has both fungible balances AND unique NFTs under the same category id. Unlike ERC-20 vs ERC-721, BCH CashTokens let one category carry both semantics simultaneously.

Typical FT+NFT pattern: a project mints a governance token (FT) alongside membership badges (NFT) under the same brand. At our last snapshot roughly 18% of the ecosystem uses this hybrid pattern — see the breakdown on /stats.
faq_q5: What's the difference between Cauldron and Tapswap?
faq_a5:
Both are places you can trade CashTokens, but they work very differently:

  • Cauldron is an AMM — a constant-product liquidity pool à la Uniswap v2. You trade against the pool at the price the pool's reserves imply; the act of trading moves the price. Listings with Cauldron presence get the C badge on the directory.
  • Tapswap is a P2P marketplace — fixed-price listings posted on-chain. No pool, no price impact; you take the listing or you don't. Listings show the T badge.

A token can be on one, both, or neither. The Venue overlap section on /stats breaks down the split.
faq_q6: What are the small icons next to each token's name?
faq_a6:
Each token in the directory shows a few compact signals next to its name:

  • FT / NFT / FT+NFT — token-type badge (what these mean).
  • Cauldron logo — the token has an active AMM pool on Cauldron with a live pool price.
  • Tapswap logo — the token has one or more open P2P listings on Tapswap. Hover the icon for the listing count.

A token can show one, both, or neither venue icon. No icons = not currently tradeable anywhere our indexer knows about.
faq_q7: What's the difference between "Tracked" and "Listed" in the header?
faq_a7:
Tracked is every CashToken category our indexer has ever seen, going back to activation block 792,772 in May 2023 — fungible and NFT, active or long-dead. It's the size of the universe we know about.

Listed is the subset that's actually tradeable right now — at least one active Cauldron pool or at least one open Tapswap listing. It's a much smaller number. The ratio (Listed ÷ Tracked) is a rough "what fraction of tokens have any liquidity?" signal.
faq_q8: Why does my token show — for Holders, UTXOs, or NFTs?
faq_a8:
Those fields come from a per-category enrichment index that joins what's unspent on chain to category membership. We're deploying it as part of the BlockBook rollout; until that's live those columns show the em-dash placeholder.

If you're seeing em-dashes and you expect real numbers, BlockBook hasn't finished its initial index build yet. Give it a day or two.
faq_q9: How often does the data refresh?
faq_a9:
Different sources update at different cadences:

  • New categories + Tapswap listings — sub-second. The tail worker subscribes to BCH node ZMQ and indexes within milliseconds of a new block.
  • Cauldron prices + TVL — every 10 minutes for already-listed tokens (~60-90s fast refresh), plus a 4-hour full scan that discovers newly-listed tokens and prunes delisted ones.
  • BCMR metadata (name, symbol, icon, description) — hourly via the on-chain authchain walker. The walker reads each category's authchain directly from BCH and sha256-verifies the publisher's JSON body against the on-chain locator before caching it. No third-party indexer.
  • BCH/USD price — every 5 minutes.
  • Sparklines + 1h/24h/7d % change — rebuilt from the price history each request; fills in over the 7 days following deploy.
faq_a10:
The tiny inline chart at the right end of each directory row — it plots the token's Cauldron price over the last 7 days as a single-line graph. Green stroke if the 7-day trend is up, rose if down, slate if flat.

Each data point is one Cauldron sync snapshot. With the worker running every 10 minutes, a fully-populated sparkline holds ~1,000 points; the SVG downsamples that to a readable line.

Tokens without a Cauldron pool (Tapswap-only or not listed) show "—" in the sparkline column — no on-chain price history means nothing to chart. The same price series drives the 1h / 24h / 7d % change columns too.
faq_q11: Why is the Market cap blank for some tokens?
faq_a11:
A token's market cap is price × circulating supply, and the price we have comes from its Cauldron AMM pool. When a pool holds only a few dollars of liquidity, a single dust-sized swap can move that price by orders of magnitude — and multiplying it by a billion-unit supply yields a "cap" that looks enormous but means nothing. Left in the rankings, those numbers shove genuinely-traded tokens down the page.

So we hide the MCap figure for any token whose Cauldron TVL is below the average TVL of the top half of currently listed tokens. The threshold is recomputed on every page load — as more deep pools get listed the bar rises; if liquidity dries up across the top end it falls. No hardcoded dollar floor to age out.

The token itself is unchanged: price, TVL, supply, holders, sparkline, and the detail page are all still shown. Only the misleading derived figure is withheld. Tokens with no Cauldron pool at all never had a derivable market cap in the first place.
faq_q12: Where do token names, symbols, and icons come from?
faq_a12:
From BCMR (the Bitcoin Cash Metadata Registries CHIP). Token creators publish a JSON manifest (name, symbol, decimals, description, icon, links, NFT schema, and more) and commit its URI + sha256 inside an OP_RETURN BCMR locator on their token's authchain.

We read it directly from the chain — our hourly sync-bcmr-onchain worker walks each category's authchain via our local BlockBook, parses the on-chain locator, fetches the publisher's URI, and sha256-verifies the body before caching it. The "BCMR JSON" link at the bottom of every token detail page points to the publisher's own URI (typically an IPFS CID or a self-hosted file), so you can read the same bytes we did. No third-party indexer in the path: if a publisher hasn't put a BCMR locator on their authchain, we don't show metadata — the bare category hex shows instead.

If a token shows a plain category hex instead of a name, the minter hasn't published an on-chain BCMR locator yet. On the token detail page you can see every BCMR field we know about under the "BCMR metadata" card.
faq_q13: Why don't I see emojis in token names?
faq_a13:
We strip emoji from token names, symbols, and descriptions at display time. In a dense directory full of rows, emoji turn into visual noise — they compete with the real name, bloat mobile line-heights, and make it hard to tell a serious project from a lookalike that copied the same glyph. Removing them keeps the grid scannable.

The on-chain data isn't touched. BCMR metadata is stored exactly as the issuer published it; the emoji filter runs only in the browser-facing render path. If you need the raw string, the /api/tokens endpoint returns it unmodified.
faq_q14: How does TokenStork filter token icons?
faq_a14:
Token icons come from BCMR metadata that issuers publish themselves — typically as ipfs:// URIs anyone on the network can fill with anything. We don't render those URLs directly in your browser; instead, every icon goes through a small safety pipeline before it's served, and only icons that clear every gate end up visible on the site.

What we check

  • Size. Anything larger than 2 MiB is rejected outright — both to keep page weight reasonable and to short-circuit decompression-bomb images.
  • Format. PNG, JPEG, WebP, and GIF first-frame are supported as static rasters. SVG is rasterized server-side via resvg before serving — your browser never sees the SVG XML, so embedded scripts, event handlers, and external href references can't reach you. Animated GIF/APNG render their first frame only.
  • Adult content. Each icon is run through Google Cloud Vision's SafeSearch classifier. Scores above a configured threshold are auto-blocked; borderline scores route to an operator-reviewed queue.
  • CSAM. Cloudflare's CSAM Scanning Tool runs against every icon the moment it transits their edge — backed by NCMEC + IWF hash databases. Matches are blocked at the network edge and reported automatically per US/EU mandatory-reporting law.

How we serve them

Icons that pass every gate are transcoded to a static WebP, content-addressed by their SHA-256 hash, and served from /icons/<hash>.webp behind a long cache. We never hot-link to the issuer's IPFS gateway from your browser — all bytes go through our origin, scanned and cached.

Why some tokens show a grey placeholder

Default-deny: until an icon is scanned and cleared, you see the placeholder. Reasons a token might still show grey:

  • The icon is queued for scanning but hasn't been processed yet (new mints flip from grey to real within ~15 minutes).
  • The fetch failed (IPFS gateway timeout) — we retry on a future tick.
  • The icon was rejected for one of the reasons above.
  • The token has no BCMR icon at all — the issuer never published one.

Privacy

The classifier sees only the icon bytes — anonymous, no user identifier, no IP, no session. Bytes are processed in transit and not retained by Google for SafeSearch calls. We keep the SHA-256 hash and a score; we don't keep the bytes ourselves once they're either cleared (transcoded copy on disk) or blocked (deleted).

Spotted an icon that looks wrong (false-blocked legitimate art, or a harmful image that slipped through)? Email hello@panmoni.com with the token's category hex and we'll triage.
faq_q15: How is the headline Total TVL computed?
faq_a15:
The Total TVL pill in the header sums BCH-side reserve across every pool on every AMM venue we index — Cauldron plus Fex.cash. The number is quoted in USD using the live BCH spot price.

The Cauldron portion comes from indexer.cauldron.quest's canonical ecosystem TVL — the same number the /stats page shows on its "Cauldron AMM" card. We cache it for 30 minutes via our sync-cauldron-stats worker so the homepage doesn't pay a network round-trip on every render.

The Fex portion is summed locally: our Fex worker enumerates every pool on the BCH chain via a scantxoutset against the AssetCovenant P2SH and stores both the per-category canonical pool (for directory price + spread display) and the sum across all pools per category (for the headline TVL).

Tapswap is deliberately excluded — its open offers are P2P intent, not pooled liquidity, so summing them as TVL would double-count or mislead. We use the conservative single-side convention everywhere (BCH-side reserve only, not the doubled both-sides figure that the wider DeFi industry typically reports).
faq_a16:
The Txs 24h pill in the header — and the matching Token-bearing txs card on /stats — counts every transaction in the last 24 hours that emits at least one CashToken-bearing output. A tx that mints a new category, transfers existing tokens, or moves multiple token types in a single tx all count as one entry. Coinbase transactions are excluded.

We derive the count purely from on-chain data — no DB lookup, no external API. Our sync-tail worker reads the verbose JSON of every new block from our local BCHN node, scans each transaction's outputs for any tokenData field, and stores a per-block tally in the blocks.token_tx_count column. The headline number is just SUM(token_tx_count) WHERE time > now() - INTERVAL '24 hours'. Roughly 144 blocks land in any 24-hour window at BCH's 10-minute target spacing, so a fresh deploy might briefly show a partial number while the rolling window fills.

The companion New categories card on /stats uses a stricter detection: per the CashTokens CHIP, a transaction creates a new token category if it spends a previous transaction's output at index 0 and emits a token_data vout whose category id matches that parent's transaction id. The category id IS the parent UTXO's txid by spec, so we can detect new categories without consulting a database — every chain participant can verify the same number.

What this number is not: it is not BCH transaction volume, not USD volume, and not a measure of trade activity specifically. A token's whole UTXO set being reorganised by its issuer counts the same as a real user-driven transfer. If you want trading-specific signal, look at the Cauldron 24h volume on /stats or Tapswap's open-listing depth.
faq_a17:
BCMR (Bitcoin Cash Metadata Registry) is how a CashToken tells wallets + explorers what its name, symbol, icon, and description are. Without a BCMR publication, your token shows up as a 16-char hex string — useless for any kind of brand or community use. The Publish BCMR wizard at tokenstork.com walks you through publishing one on-chain.

Who can publish? Only the wallet holding your category's authority NFT — the NFT created at genesis that anchors the authchain. The wizard automatically detects which categories your connected wallet can publish for and rejects anything else. This is the CashTokens-protocol way of preventing impersonation: if someone else minted the token, they can't publish metadata on your behalf, and vice versa.

How does the wizard work? Six steps:

  1. Enter the identity: name, ticker (symbol), decimals, description.
  2. Paste an icon URI (https:// or ipfs://) or skip.
  3. We generate the canonical BCMR JSON and compute its sha256 — that hash will be committed on-chain alongside your publication URL.
  4. Upload the JSON to your own IPFS / Pinata / Lighthouse / any HTTPS host, paste the URL, and we sha256-verify the returned bytes. Your own host is the canonical source. Optionally, opt into a tokenstork-hosted backup mirror (operator-approved; serves at https://tokenstork.com/bcmr/<hash>.json as a fallback).
  5. We build the unsigned transaction that spends your authority NFT, emits a new one (preserving the NFT's commitment + capability), and attaches the OP_RETURN BCMR locator with your content hash + URL. You sign in your wallet and paste the signed hex.
  6. We broadcast to BCH. Done.

What happens after broadcast? Our on-chain BCMR walker runs hourly, and on its next tick it'll see your new authchain hop, fetch the JSON from your host, verify the hash matches, and update the token's metadata across the directory. Typically your token's name and icon appear within an hour of broadcast.

Why does my IPFS host need to keep the JSON? The on-chain locator points at your URL — that's the canonical reference for the metadata. If your host goes down, any cached copy (including ours, if you opted in) still works, but the publisher-of-record is you. Start a publication →
faq_q18: How do I add a token? How do I report one?
faq_a18:
Add: nothing to do! We index every CashToken category automatically. Once you mint a token it appears on tokenstork within seconds. To show a name and icon, publish BCMR metadata via your authchain — Paytaca's wallet has built-in tools for this.

Report: every token detail page has a "Report this token" button below the stats. Use it for spam, phishing, fraud, or deliberately offensive content. Reports go directly to us, not the token creator.
faq_q19: How are tokens ranked on the up/down vote leaderboards?
faq_a19:
The "Most upvoted / downvoted / controversial" leaderboards on the homepage don't just count raw ↑ and ↓ clicks. Each vote contributes a weighted score, and the leaderboard sorts on the sum of those weights. The directory's ?sort=upvoted / downvoted / controversial options use the same weights.

Two things shape a single vote's weight:

  1. How long the voter has been around. Each wallet's tenure is the number of distinct UTC days on which it has cast at least one vote action. A vote from a brand-new wallet contributes 1.0; a vote from a wallet that's been active 30 days contributes 5.0; a year-tenured wallet contributes about 8.5. Growth is logarithmic (log₂(tenure_days + 2)) — established voters get more say, but a tireless contributor doesn't dominate.
  2. How recently the vote was cast. Vote weight halves every 7 days (0.5age_days / 7). A vote cast today is worth 1.0×, last week's is worth 0.5×, two weeks old is 0.25×, a month-old vote is about 0.05×. Old enthusiasm fades; the leaderboard reflects the current mood.

Multiplied together, the formula favours consistent votes from established voters, and naturally pushes raid-style bursts of fresh wallets toward the bottom of the ranking. The raw ↑ / ↓ counts shown next to each token are still the simple totals — only the ordering uses the weighted score.

Each wallet may cast, change, or retract at most 20 vote actions per UTC day. That's well above any organic per-day activity but tight enough that a script-controlled wallet can't shove a token to the top in one sitting. The counter resets at 00:00 UTC.
faq_a20:
Sign in with your wallet, navigate to a token you hold (any FT balance or NFT counts), and click Airdrop on the token detail page. Pick a recipient token — the wizard will airdrop your token to every wallet that currently holds the recipient token.

Two split modes. Equal divides your total amount evenly across all recipients (truncation residue stays with you). Weighted sends each recipient a share proportional to their balance + NFT count of the recipient token. Sender's own wallet is always filtered out of the recipient list.

Recipient lists come from our local token_holders snapshot (refreshed every 6 h by the enrich worker), so a brand-new token minted in the last few hours may not have all its holders indexed yet. The wizard surfaces the snapshot timestamp so you can decide whether to wait.

Big airdrops are split into chunks of up to 600 recipients per transaction. Your wallet signs each chunk in sequence (paste the signed hex back into the wizard); a wallet rejection on chunk K leaves earlier chunks intact and lets you retry K from the receipt page. If our holder snapshot advances mid-airdrop (an enrich tick fires), remaining chunks halt with a "redraft" prompt rather than send to a possibly-stale set.

Privacy heads-up: like every UTXO transaction, an airdrop reveals your sender wallet to every recipient via the tx-input. Your wallet's public key hash is visible in any block explorer.
faq_a21: Yes. Code is at github.com/Panmoni/tokenstork. Pull requests and issues welcome. The /roadmap shows what's shipped and what's next.
home_sentiment_tip_text: Ranks weight votes by voter tenure × recency (7-day half-life).
home_grid_retry: Please try again in a moment.
grid_search_ph: Search name, symbol, description, or category...
grid_filter_cauldron_title: Only show tokens currently listed on the Cauldron DEX
grid_filter_tapswap_title: Only show tokens with open P2P listings on Tapswap
grid_filter_fex_title: Only show tokens currently listed on Fex.cash (AMM)
grid_col_1h_title: Price change vs. the nearest history point ≥1h old. Cauldron syncs every 4h, so this often reflects the ~4h mark.
grid_col_24h_title: Price change vs. the nearest history point ≥24h old
grid_col_7d_title: Price change vs. the nearest history point ≥7d old
grid_col_holders_title: Distinct addresses currently holding this category, per the latest BlockBook enrichment pass.
grid_bcmr_suspect_title: BCMR metadata is unstable: the current metadata was pulled, a publication failed to verify, or the authority key moved recently. See the token page for details.
grid_first_n_title: Permanent rank: this is the {label} minted on Bitcoin Cash.
grid_age_warning: {label} — brand-new categories are over-represented in scams and abandoned tests; verify before trusting.
grid_crc20_symbol_title: On-chain CRC-20 symbol (no BCMR symbol published)
grid_empty_body: Try a different search or filter.
star_title_add: Click to add to your watchlist
crc20_tip_canonical: CRC-20 — owns the symbol "{symbol}" on-chain via covenant
crc20_tip_noncanonical: CRC-20 — also claims "{symbol}" but lost the canonical sort to an earlier genesis
movers_tip_text: Biggest 24-hour price + TVL changes among Cauldron-listed tokens. Computed from the oldest price_history point ≥23h ago vs. the newest point within the last 23h. Tokens without two qualifying points (newly listed, or sync gap) are excluded.
movers_none_up: No tokens up in the last 24h.
movers_none_down: No tokens down in the last 24h.
movers_none_tvl: No measurable TVL changes in the last 24h.
wl_intro: Tokens you're tracking, scoped to the BCH wallet you signed in with. Click the star on any token in the directory or detail page to add or remove.
wl_signin_h2: Sign in with your BCH wallet to start tracking
wl_signin_body: The watchlist is scoped to the wallet address you sign in with — no email, no password, no recovery phrase to lose. Cross-device by design: sign in from any browser with the same wallet and your watchlist follows.
wl_empty_before: Click the star icon next to any token in the
wl_empty_after: or on a token's detail page to add it here.
adl_empty: You haven't started any airdrops yet.
adl_empty_cta: Browse the directory to find a token you hold
mints_empty: You haven't minted any tokens yet.
mod_meta_description: Public list of CashTokens hidden from the Token Stork directory, with the reason and date for each.
mod_intro_1: These categories are hidden from the directory, the public API, and the stats counters. Direct URLs return
mod_intro_2: We list them here so you can see what we filter and why. To report a token, use the report link on its detail page.
mod_img_desc_before: Every token icon is fetched, hashed, and scanned before it's served. We block adult content and CSAM (the latter via Cloudflare's edge-resident NCMEC/IWF hash matcher), reject oversize files (> 2 MiB) and non-raster formats (SVG, AVIF, ICO, …), and route borderline scores to an operator review queue. Cleared icons are transcoded to static WebP and served from our origin — never hot-linked from issuer-controlled URLs. See the
mod_img_footer: {rendered} tokens render a real WebP in the directory today; {blocked} unique images blocked total ({pending} URLs still pending fetch / retry).
mod_none_body: Spot something that should be? Use the report link on any token detail page.
crc_meta_description: CRC-20 tokens on Bitcoin Cash — names claimed via covenant in the genesis transaction, with deterministic per-symbol canonical winners detected from on-chain data.
crc_intro: CRC-20 is a permissionless naming convention on top of Bitcoin Cash CashTokens. A token's symbol, decimals, and name are encoded inside a 21-byte covenant in the genesis transaction. A deterministic per-symbol sort picks one canonical winner per symbol bucket — the rest are non-canonical contenders. Every detection on this page is derived from on-chain data alone. Reference →
crc_contested_desc_before: Symbols claimed by more than one CashTokens category. The canonical-winner rule (lowest
crc_contested_desc_after: , breaking ties by category id then input index) picks exactly one as authoritative; the rest are still detectable but lose the symbol claim.
crc_none_before: No CRC-20 categories detected yet. Run
td_bcmr_json_title: Open the on-chain-published BCMR JSON (the publisher's own copy)
td_bcmr_gateway_title: View via ipfs.io public gateway (HTTPS proxy for the ipfs:// link)
td_airdrop_title: Airdrop a token you hold to all holders of this token
td_update_bcmr_title: Update this category's BCMR metadata. You hold the authority NFT.
td_publish_bcmr_title: Publish BCMR metadata so this token displays a name, symbol, icon, and description. You hold the authority NFT.
td_verified_tip: Confirmed on-chain via our local BCHN: this category id appears in a real CashTokens genesis transaction at the genesis block, and current supply / live UTXOs / holders are derived from the indexed UTXO set (not from a third-party indexer or self-reported metadata).
td_minting_tip: At least one live UTXO of this category carries the `minting` NFT capability. Whoever holds that UTXO can mint additional NFTs of this category at any time. Supply is not capped at the level shown — treat the supply number as a snapshot rather than the maximum.
td_invalid_splitid_title: Invalid hex splitId — not rendered as link
td_crc20_vs_bcmr_q: What's the difference between CRC-20 and BCMR?
td_crc20_subtitle: On-chain naming claim via covenant in genesis transaction.
td_canonical_winner_title: This category is the canonical winner for "{symbol}" — the earliest valid genesis under the per-symbol sort.
td_noncanonical_title: This category claims the same symbol but lost the per-symbol canonical sort to an earlier genesis.
td_nonutf8_title: Symbol bytes are not valid UTF-8; rendered as hex.
td_fair_genesis_label: How fair genesis height is computed
td_fair_genesis_text: max(commit_block, reveal_block - 20). Drives the per-symbol canonical sort.
td_first_n_title: Permanent rank: this is the {label} minted on Bitcoin Cash, ordered by (genesis block, category hex).
td_age_window_title: This category was minted on-chain within the warning window.
td_cauldron_share_title: This token's Cauldron pool TVL is {pct}% of the entire Cauldron exchange.
td_standing_title: Ranked #{rank} in {bucket} on {day}. Click to view leaderboards.
td_tvl_mover_title: Top {n} of 5 24h TVL movers on Cauldron
td_arbitrage_title: Listed on {venues} venues — visible on the /arbitrage page
td_watchlist_count_title: Number of distinct wallets that have added this token to their watchlist
td_icon_adjusted_1: the publisher's BCMR icon was out of standard (
td_icon_adjusted_2: ); TokenStork transcoded it to display safely.
td_distribution_tip: Gini coefficient of the holder fungible-balance distribution. 0 = perfectly equal; 1 = one address owns everything. Exchange covenants count as single 'holders' here. Suppressed for fewer than 10 holders and pure-NFT collections.
td_market_cap_title: Hidden for tokens whose Cauldron TVL is below the average TVL of the top half of listed tokens.
td_live_utxos_tip: Number of currently-unspent on-chain outputs that carry this token category. Counted by our local BlockBook indexer.
td_live_nfts_tip: Number of unspent NFT outputs of this category. Each NFT in CashTokens is a UTXO with non-empty commitment data.
td_top_holder_title: Largest single holder's balance ÷ current supply
td_hhi_tip: Herfindahl-Hirschman Index across the full holder set. Sum of squared shares. Range [1/N, 1]. Suppressed for fewer than 10 holders.
td_open_reports_title: Number of unactioned user reports against this token
td_24h_activity_title: Number of price-history buckets in the last 24h with non-zero TVL delta — proxy for trade activity
td_trading_note: Volume is a lower-bound estimate from |TVL deltas|; price extremes are sampled at our 4 h Cauldron sync cadence.
td_chart_note: Volume is a lower-bound estimate from |TVL deltas| between consecutive snapshots — within-bucket round-trip activity isn't visible at our 4 h sync cadence (10 min fast-pass for already-listed tokens). Price is the per-bucket mean.
td_chart_error: The price chart couldn't load just now — refresh in a moment to try again.
td_maker_title: Maker public-key hash (raw bytes; cashaddr rendering deferred)
td_tapswap_note: Listings aggregated from on-chain MPSW OP_RETURNs via our own BCHN — not from Tapswap's API. Close events (sale / cancellation) are not tracked yet; a stale listing that's already been taken will drop off once that enhancement ships.
td_report_thanks_body: Your report has been recorded. The operator will triage it shortly.
td_report_intro: Flag content you believe violates good-faith use of the directory. Your report is anonymous by default; leave an email only if you'd like a follow-up.
td_report_details_ph: Why is this token problematic? Any context you can share helps us triage.
td_report_ratelimited: You've submitted several reports recently. Please wait a bit before sending another.
td_report_error: Couldn't submit right now. Please try again in a moment.
mn_meta_description: BCH mining pool attribution + fee market dynamics, derived from on-chain coinbase scriptSig analysis since CashTokens activation.
mn_intro: Mining-pool attribution + fee market dynamics for Bitcoin Cash since CashTokens activation (block 792,772). Pool identity is decoded from each block's coinbase scriptSig — the same byte-pattern matching every BCH explorer does.
mn_no_blocks: No blocks in this window yet.
mn_note1: Pool attribution is decoded from each block's coinbase scriptSig via byte-substring matching against a hand-curated list of pool tags (/ViaBTC/, /AntPool/, Foundry USA Pool, etc.). Solo miners and unrecognized tags bucket as Unknown.
mn_note2: Fee rate is sats / byte computed from the day's total fees ÷ total block bytes. It's a proxy for the fee market — actual mempool fee rates are higher because empty space in low-utilization blocks brings the average down.
mn_note3: Miner take per block is the coinbase output value (subsidy + fees). Today the BCH subsidy is fixed at 6.25 BCH/block until the next halving in 2028; fee revenue is a small fraction on top.
mn_note4: Coverage starts at block 792,772 (CashTokens activation, May 2023). Pre-activation history is out of scope for this site.
arb_meta_description: BCH CashTokens listed on multiple venues (Cauldron, Fex, Tapswap), ranked by raw price spread. Informational only — slippage, mempool risk, and execution cost not modelled.
arb_intro: Tokens listed on at least two of three venues — Cauldron AMM, Fex AMM, and Tapswap P2P — with a meaningful price gap between them. The "net" column deducts a per-row fee specific to the cheapest-vs-most-expensive venue pair (see notes). Slippage, mining cost, and the chance the gap closes before you act are NOT modelled.
arb_bch_unavailable: BCH price feed is unavailable, so USD columns show — for now. Spread % is independent of the BCH price and is still accurate; refresh the page in a moment.
arb_empty: No tokens currently meet the {pct}% spread filter.
arb_empty_2_before: {total} tokens are listed on at least two venues. Try the
arb_col_spread_title: Absolute price gap, ignoring fees and slippage
arb_col_net_title: Spread minus the venue-pair-specific round-trip taker fee
arb_note1: Cauldron / Fex prices come from token_venue_listings; Tapswap price is the lowest want_sats / has_amount across open FT-only listings (NFTs are excluded — different price semantics). Refresh cadence: Cauldron 4 h discovery + 10 min fast-pass; Fex 4 h; Tapswap is event-driven, near real-time.
arb_note2: Per-venue taker fees — buy leg (when you fill): Cauldron {buyC}% / Fex {buyF}% / Tapswap {buyT}% (the 3% Tapswap platform fee is paid by the maker out of want_sats, not the taker). Sell leg (when you create the listing): Cauldron {sellC}% / Fex {sellF}% / Tapswap {sellT}%. The Net column subtracts whichever pair this row's cheapest-vs-most-expensive venues actually use.
arb_note3: Slippage on thin pools (or absorbing all listings on Tapswap) eats the spread before you do. Always sanity-check against the live venue UI before committing funds.
arb_note4: Tapswap NFT listings are tracked but not surfaced here — each NFT is unique by commitment and "lowest ask" doesn't aggregate cleanly across them. Day-1 is FT-only; NFT-specific arbitrage is a tracked follow-up.
arb_note5: tokenstork.com displays public market data; nothing here is investment advice or an offer.
bl_meta_description: Per-block economics dashboard for Bitcoin Cash from CashTokens activation forward: tx count, miner take, fees, total economic value transferred, block size.
bl_intro: Per-block economics for Bitcoin Cash from CashTokens activation (block 792,772) onward. Each row summarizes a single block: how many transactions it contains, what the miner collected, how much of that was fees vs. the protocol subsidy, and the total economic value transferred across non-coinbase outputs.
bl_value_title: Sum of outputs across non-coinbase txs
bl_fee_value_title: Miner fees as a percentage of total economic value transferred
bl_empty: No blocks indexed yet. The backfill will populate this page in ~30 minutes; the live tail walker keeps it current from there.
bl_note1: Miner take is the sum of the coinbase transaction's outputs — what the block's miner actually claimed. It equals the protocol subsidy plus all transaction fees, and a miner can legally claim less (any unclaimed sats are burned).
bl_note2: Fees are derived as miner_take − subsidy_at_height, where the subsidy follows the BCH halving schedule (50 BCH from genesis, halving every 210,000 blocks). At the current era subsidy is 6.25 BCH per block.
bl_note3: Economic value is the sum of every output across the block's non-coinbase transactions. It overstates "value transferred" because most txs include change-back-to-self outputs — but it's a useful, deterministic-from-the-block lens without needing to fetch every prev-output to compute net flows.
bl_note4: Block hashes link out to salemkode.com for full transaction-level detail. tokenstork.com indexes the per-block summary; the explorer has the per-tx breakdown.
bl_note5: Coverage starts at block 792,772 (CashTokens activation, May 2023). Pre-activation history is out of scope.
st_meta_description: Ecosystem-level statistics for the BCH CashTokens directory.
st_intro: Headline numbers for the BCH CashTokens ecosystem, computed directly from the tokens we've indexed.
st_pool_price_sub: distinct tokens with an active pool price
st_top_holders_desc: Distinct on-chain addresses holding the category. Exchange covenants (Cauldron pool UTXOs, Tapswap escrow, Fex covenant) each count as a single holder, so actively-traded tokens may be slightly understated vs. truly-distributed tokens of the same headline number.
st_tapswap_top_desc: Where Tapswap's P2P liquidity is concentrated. Counts open listings only — closed/taken offers don't count. Reflects "this token is actively being offered" rather than "this token has ever been on Tapswap."
st_cauldron_amm_desc: Live aggregates from indexer.cauldron.quest. TVL is the BCH-side reserve only — conservative (industry convention doubles this to count the token side too). Volumes are sampled at page render.
st_cauldron_chart_aria: Cumulative unique Cauldron addresses by month
st_growth_desc: New categories minted each month since CashTokens activation. Bucketed by on-chain genesis block timestamp — {count} tokens across the full history.
st_venue_overlap_desc: How the indexed tokens distribute across our three trading venues. The pair / triple intersections are the natural targets for cross-venue arbitrage — same token, different prices on each.
st_all_three_title: Best arbitrage surface — three-way price comparison
st_metadata_desc: What fraction of the {count} indexed tokens publish each BCMR field. Empty strings count as missing — the directory treats them that way for display.
st_ecosystem_tvl_desc: Daily mean BCH-side reserve summed across every Cauldron + Fex pool we index. Lines move with both pool inflows/outflows AND BCH-price-driven token-side rebalancing — this is conservative single-side TVL, not the doubled industry convention. Tapswap (P2P) is deliberately excluded.
st_no_history_price: No history yet — the price-history table is still accumulating points.
st_supply_desc: How fungible (FT and FT+NFT) tokens distribute by displayable supply (current_supply ÷ 10^decimals). Buckets are powers of ten; "zero / unknown" includes tokens we haven't enriched yet.
st_decimals_desc: How FT and FT+NFT tokens choose their decimals. Most cash-token communities settle on a small set of canonical values; deviations often signal copy-paste configs.
st_gini_median_tooltip: Median Gini across all tokens with at least 10 holders. Reported as median (not mean) because the directory's distribution is heavily right-skewed — a handful of single-whale categories pull the mean toward 1.0 and obscure the typical token's score.
st_gini_desc: Gini coefficient measures how unequally a token's supply is split across its holders. 0 = perfectly equal; 1 = one address owns everything. Crypto distributions concentrate harder than country-income distributions — Bitcoin's holder-Gini is around 0.97 — so the tier cutoffs are calibrated for that reality. Excluded: tokens with fewer than 10 holders (where the math produces meaningless extremes).
st_gini_tier_title: Show all {count} tokens in the "{label}" Gini tier
st_eco_holders_desc: Distinct on-chain addresses that hold at least one CashToken category, plus the top collectors ranked by category count. Exchange covenants (Cauldron pool UTXOs, Tapswap escrow, Fex covenant) show up as single addresses here, so true unique-user count is somewhat lower than the headline number — the same caveat as the per-token Gini score.
st_distinct_addresses: Distinct addresses holding at least one category
st_top_collectors: Top 10 collectors — by distinct categories held
st_supply_dyn_desc: Two ends of the supply-mutability spectrum. Fully-burned categories are permanently frozen at zero circulating supply. Active-minting categories still hold a live minting NFT and the issuer can mint more tomorrow — relevant to anyone weighing whether a "capped supply" claim is real.
st_supply_dyn_pending: Supply dynamics are enriched from live UTXO counts — this requires our BlockBook indexer, which is not yet deployed. Numbers will appear here once the enrichment worker has run.
st_fully_burned_sub: Supply locked at zero, no UTXOs left
st_active_minting_title: Categories with at least one live minting NFT — issuer can still mint additional supply.
st_active_minting_sub: Supply still expandable by the issuer
st_activity_desc: CashToken activity in the last 24 hours, summed across the {count} blocks our tail walker has processed in that window. Token transfers counts every block tx that emits at least one token-bearing output (transfers, mints, multi-token movements — each tx counts once). New categories counts genesis txs (the spec-required pattern: spend index-0 of a prior output AND emit a token whose category equals the spending tx's own txid). Both numbers are pure on-chain — no DB lookup, no external API, derived directly from the verbose block JSON.
st_activity_note: Note: only {count} of an expected ~144 blocks are present in this window; the activity counters are still accruing since the schema migration. Run blocks-backfill to populate historical blocks retroactively, or wait ~24h for the tail to fill the rolling window.
st_token_bearing_title: Number of txs in the last 24h that emit at least one token_data output. Coinbase excluded.
st_mints_transfers_sub: Mints + transfers, each tx counted once
st_new_categories_title: New categories minted in the last 24h, detected via the CashTokens genesis pattern (vin[0] spends index-0; emitted token's category == own txid).
st_first_created_desc: The earliest CashTokens categories minted on chain, ordered by genesis block. The oldest of the old — many predate widespread tooling, so metadata may be sparse.
st_moderation_desc: Categories filtered out of every other counter on this page. Hidden from the directory and the public API; we publish the list for transparency.
st_moderated_sub: see the list with reason and date
st_icon_safety_desc: Every BCMR-supplied token icon is scanned for adult content + CSAM, capped at 2 MiB, transcoded to static WebP, and served from our origin (never hot-linked). Counts below are per unique image hash — a single hash can back many tokens.
st_footer_note: Counts reflect what our indexer has seen since CashTokens activation at block 792,772 (May 2023). BCMR metadata is read directly from each category's on-chain authchain by our sync-bcmr-onchain worker (hourly), which sha256-verifies the publisher's JSON body against the on-chain locator before caching it.
td_trust_tooltip_fallback: How long this token's BCMR metadata has been stable, and whether its identity or authority key has changed.
td_trust_reason_pulled: current metadata was pulled or stopped verifying
td_trust_reason_never_verified: no publication has ever verified against its on-chain hash
td_bcmr_history_intro: Every metadata publication we've archived for this token, newest first. Each snapshot is immutable from our side; the pin link returns that exact version as of its block.
td_bcmr_pin_title: Fetch this exact BCMR version as of its block
td_bcmr_oversize: body too large to archive inline
